title: ATDD followed: acceptance suite drives implementation through the public MCP interface only
evidence: test/bsoccer_acceptance_tests.erl:337-365 — rpc/call/raw_call touch the system solely via bsoccer_mcp:handle/1 (JSON-RPC); no ETS/internal back-door. 20 scenarios assert domain outcomes (WHAT), with unit TDD underneath in bsoccer_csv_tests.erl / bsoccer_norm_tests.erl.
suggestion: None — methodology adherence is strong; recorded for cross-prompt comparison.

title: Extended match stats (shots/corners) are loaded but never surfaced by any tool
evidence: src/bsoccer_data.erl:183-186 stores home_shots/away_shots/home_corner/away_corner for BR-Football rows, but no tool in src/bsoccer_query.erl exposes them; aggregate_statistics only uses goals.
suggestion: Either expose shots/corners via aggregate_statistics or drop the unused parse work.

title: token_efficiency response scored 0.0 (verbose run), not a code defect
evidence: scores.json: token_efficiency=0.0 while test_coverage=1.0, code_quality=1.0, defect_rate=1.0. The implementation is correct and complete; only token spend was high.
suggestion: No code change needed; relevant only to the prompt-methodology cost comparison.
